one_line_takeaway: Binary vulnerability-detection accuracy in LLMs is a poor proxy for genuine causal understanding -- over a third of 'correct' verdicts rest on flawed reasoning that collapses under semantic-preserving code rewrites -- and DAGVUL's DAG-structured RLVR closes much of that gap, letting an 8B model approach Claude-Sonnet-4.5's reasoning-F1 (75.47% vs 76.11%).
key_finding: Binary detection accuracy substantially overstates true reasoning quality: 36.4% of correct binary verdicts stem from hallucinatory or irrelevant reasoning ('Flawed Reasoning, Correct Answer'); for premier models (Grok-4, Gemini-2.5-Pro, DeepSeek-R1) Reasoning F1 (RF1) falls from over 60% (detection) to under 50% once reasoning is verified, and for Llama-3.1-8B RF1 drops below 10%. Pair-wise (vuln + patch) consistency is low: even top reasoners like Grok-4 and DeepSeek-R1 achieve (MATCH,MATCH) consistency below 30%. Under semantic-preserving perturbations, average RF1 drops 26.7% with Basic prompts (e.g., gemini-2.5-pro RF1 69.09% -> 22.86%; grok-4 69.16% -> 23.64%; per Table 4), a decline mitigated to an average 14.5% drop under CWE-guided prompts. Manual open-coding of 200 erroneous traces yields a taxonomy of 12 failure modes across 4 categories (Analysis Focus, Code Comprehension [CS1-4], Logic/Reasoning [AR1-4], Generative Bias [GB1-3]), with errors concentrated in the code-comprehension and logic-analysis categories rather than pure hallucination. DAGVUL raises reasoning performance: on Qwen3-8B, Basic-prompt RF1 reaches 70.97% vs. Simple baseline 53.91%, SFT 63.37%, ORPO 62.25% (RLVR yields a 7.6-point absolute RF1 gain over the SFT-only DAG variant, 70.97% vs 63.37%); RP reaches 91.67% on Qwen3-8B. On Llama3.1-Nemo-8B, DAGVUL RF1 reaches 61.05% (Basic) and 72.90% (CWE-guided). DAGVUL's 8B models beat Qwen3-8B-Reasoning (best RF1 67.23%), GPT-OSS-20B (67.96% via RAG) and Qwen3-30B-Reasoning (69.39% via DAG); under CWE-guided prompting, Qwen3-8B+DAGVUL reaches RF1 75.47%, nearly matching Claude-Sonnet-4.5's best configuration (76.11%). Logical-closeness rate rises from 32.90% to 73.25% (Qwen3-8B) and 44.74% to 67.99% (Llama-Nemo-8B) after DAGVUL training. Overall average RF1 improvement over all baselines: 18.9%.
core_contribution: The paper's own contribution is threefold: (1) the first large-scale empirical study of LLM 'vulnerability reasoning' (as opposed to binary detection), which redefines the task as predicting a tuple (Y, R) -- a verdict Y plus a causal reasoning trace R -- and requires 'Reasoning Success' to satisfy both a correct verdict AND a verified reasoning trace; (2) a new benchmark comprising a real-world dataset (5,078 CVEs across 149 CWE types, with 4,998 fine-grained ground-truth causal reports built via multimodal GPT-5 aggregation of CVE descriptions, commit diffs, GitHub issues/PRs and screenshots) and a semantic-preserving perturbation dataset built from the Juliet Test Suite (26 transformation types, 100 manually annotated critical samples across 5 CWEs: CWE-78, 190, 400, 416, 476); and (3) DAGVUL, a novel framework that reformulates vulnerability reasoning as generation of a Directed Acyclic Graph (source nodes -> intermediate inference nodes -> dual sink nodes: verified_sink / sanitized_sink) rather than linear chain-of-thought, trained via a two-stage pipeline of cold-start SFT (on a Gold Standard DAGVUL dataset of N=8,157: 4,041 vulnerability + 4,116 patch samples) followed by RLVR using GRPO (rollout size 16, reward = weighted combination of rule-based logical-closeness and a DeepSeek-R1-judged reasoning-correctness signal) on a refined 2,178-sample subset.
threat_model: Not an attacker/defender adversarial threat model in the classical sense; the paper's concern is the reliability of LLMs as automated vulnerability-reasoning/auditing systems. The core risk it targets is that an LLM auditor can emit a correct binary verdict ('vulnerable'/'not vulnerable') while its underlying causal reasoning is hallucinated, irrelevant, or based on superficial pattern matching rather than sound program-analysis logic ('Flawed Reasoning, Correct Answer') -- which the authors argue undermines trust and can propagate errors into downstream tasks like automated vulnerability repair. It also considers robustness to semantic-preserving code perturbations (i.e., whether reasoning quality is an artifact of memorized syntactic patterns/data leakage rather than genuine semantic understanding), and covers 5 concrete CWE classes (CWE-78 OS command injection, CWE-190 integer overflow, CWE-400 uncontrolled resource consumption, CWE-416 use-after-free, CWE-476 NULL pointer dereference) in the perturbation set, plus 149 CWE types in the real-world dataset.
limitations: The paper's own text surfaces several boundaries (not exhaustive citation of critique from elsewhere): the DAG gold-standard construction relies on CoT trajectories from a single teacher model (DeepSeek-R1) and an example DAG topology that was manually curated by the authors rather than fully automated; the reasoning-correctness reward in RLVR is itself judged by another LLM (DeepSeek-R1), making the reward signal model-dependent rather than a ground-truth program-analysis oracle; the automated GPT-5 judge for reasoning MATCH/MISMATCH, while validated via manual audit down to a 3% error rate, is still an LLM-based evaluator layered on an LLM-based ground-truth-report generator (also GPT-5), raising potential for correlated judge/annotator bias; RQ1/RQ2 sample sizes are modest (114 real-world samples, 100 perturbed pairs) relative to the full benchmark; training methods (RAG/SFT/ORPO/DAGVUL) were applied only to standard (non-reasoning) base models to avoid interference from built-in <think> tags, so head-to-head training comparisons against reasoning models are not run; RAG is shown to be unstable and sometimes underperforms the raw baseline, which the authors attribute to noisy retrieval but do not further mitigate.

datasets_used: Real-world dataset: 5,078 CVEs / 149 CWE types, built from PrimeVul, ReposVul, R2Vul with Joern/Cflow context enrichment; 4,998 fine-grained ground-truth causal reports (GPT-5 multimodal annotation)

Semantic-preserving Perturbation Dataset: built from the Juliet Test Suite, 26 transformation types, 100 manually-annotated critical samples across CWE-78, CWE-190, CWE-400, CWE-416, CWE-476 (20 each)
Gold Standard DAGVUL Dataset: N=8,157 total (4,041 vulnerability samples, 4,116 patch samples); refined RL subset of 2,178 samples (4,096-token context filter)
SVEN dataset (9 CWEs) used as an out-of-domain generalization test for RQ4, 114 unseen instances after excluding training-overlapping commits
RQ1 evaluation subset: 114 real-world samples; RQ2 evaluation subset: 100 perturbed pairs
quantitative_results: 36.4% of correct binary verdicts stem from hallucinatory/irrelevant reasoning ('Flawed Reasoning, Correct Answer')
RF1 for premier models drops from >60% (detection) to <50% (verified reasoning); Llama-3.1-8B RF1 <10%
Pair-wise (vuln+patch) MATCH,MATCH consistency <30% even for Grok-4 and DeepSeek-R1
Average RF1 drop under semantic perturbation: 26.7% (Basic prompt), 14.5% (CWE-guided prompt)
Table 4 examples (Original -> Rephrased RF1, Basic prompt): gemini-2.5-pro 69.09 -> 22.86; grok-4 69.16 -> 23.64; deepseek-r1 75.00 -> 57.83; qwq-32b 89.13 -> 72.09
Automated GPT-5 judge error rate after manual audit of 200 flagged instances: 3%
12 failure modes identified via open coding of 200 erroneous traces, grouped into 4 categories
DAGVUL on Qwen3-8B (Basic prompt): RF1 70.97% vs Simple 53.91%, SFT 63.37%, ORPO 62.25%; RP 91.67%
RLVR vs SFT-only DAG variant on Qwen3-8B: +7.6 absolute RF1 points (70.97% vs 63.37%)
DAGVUL (Qwen3-8B) beats Qwen3-8B-Reasoning (best RF1 67.23%), GPT-OSS-20B (67.96% via RAG), Qwen3-30B-Reasoning (69.39% via DAG)
Qwen3-8B+DAGVUL under CWE-guided prompting: RF1 75.47%, vs Claude-Sonnet-4.5 best configuration RF1 76.11%
Average RF1 improvement of DAGVUL over all baselines: 18.9%
Logical closeness rate after DAGVUL training: Qwen3-8B 32.90% -> 73.25%; Llama-Nemo-8B 44.74% -> 67.99%
baselines_compared: Detection-only binary classification (Accuracy/Precision/Recall/F1) vs reasoning-aware metrics (RAcc/RP/RR/RF1)
Simple/Basic inference and CoT inference strategies
RAG (Vul-RAG-style retrieval on SVEN training corpus)
DAG-only (SFT on DAG format without RLVR) as an ablation of DAGVUL
Reasoning-specialized large models: Qwen3-30B-Reasoning, GPT-OSS-20B-High, Qwen3-8B-Reasoning
10 SOTA LLMs in the empirical study: qwen2.5:7b, llama3.1:8b, llama3.2:3b, gpt-3.5, deepseek-v3, claude-4-sonnet (standard); qwq-32b, deepseek-r1, gemini-2.5-pro, grok-4 (reasoning)
